title: indigofera neoglabra
intro: Indigofera neoglabra, also known as the Indian snake root or neoglabra, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Indigofera neoglabra include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In herbal preparations, the roots of Indigofera neoglabra are often made into a decoction or powder, which can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies.
